title: Renting a Bike in Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **There are four ways to rent a bike in Berlin**, and the cheapest one depends entirely on your trip: free-floating e-bikes, Nextbike, day rentals, and monthly subscriptions.
**Free-floating e-bikes (Bolt, Dott, Lime)** are unlocked by app and left anywhere. Expect about a 1 EUR unlock plus a per-minute charge, so they are great for a quick hop and pricey for long rides.
**Nextbike is the cheapest classic pedal bike**, around 1 EUR per 15 minutes with day passes. Watch the rule: finishing outside the S-Bahn ring away from a station adds about a 20 EUR fee.
**For a full relaxed day, use Donkey Republic's day pass (from about 12 EUR) or a bike-shop day rental (about 10 to 15 EUR)**, which comes with a lock and no app to babysit.
**Swapfiets, the blue-tyre subscription bikes, only make sense if you are staying for several weeks or longer.**
**My rule of thumb:** use a bike for the big flat distances like Tiergarten or Tempelhofer Feld, then walk the dense historic centre, where the point is the streets themselves.
